name: Ahom Dynasty

rulers.name: Suhungmung
rulers.notes: Adopted Hindu name Swarganarayan; expanded kingdom

rulers.name: Lachit Borphukan (general)
rulers.notes: Naval victory at Saraighat — repelled Mughals (not a king)

rulers.name: Purandar Singha
rulers.notes: Last; Yandabo treaty ceded Assam to British

religion: Tai animism initially; Hinduism (Shaiva, Vaishnava) from 16th c.
notable_achievements: Successfully resisted Mughal expansion for centuries (notably Battle of Saraighat 1671)
~600-year continuous dynasty — among longest in world history
Ahom Buranjis (historical chronicles)
notes: Partial ruler list; ~40 Ahom kings in total.

name: Bahmani Sultanate

rulers.name: Kalimullah Shah
rulers.notes: Last; sultanate fragmented into Deccan sultanates

notable_achievements: First independent Muslim sultanate of the Deccan
Patronage of Persian and Dakhni Urdu
Madrasa of Mahmud Gawan at Bidar
notes: Fragmented into the five Deccan Sultanates (Ahmadnagar, Bijapur, Berar, Golconda, Bidar) c. 1490–1527.

name: British Raj

rulers.name: George V
rulers.notes: Delhi Durbar 1911; capital moved to New Delhi

rulers.name: George VI
rulers.notes: Last Emperor of India; partition and independence

religion: Christianity (Church of England, official); religious pluralism in practice
notable_achievements: Pan-Indian railways, post, telegraph, codified law
Severe famines and economic extraction; basis of large modern historiography
notes: East India Company rule (1757–1858) preceded Crown rule; this record covers Crown Raj only. Sovereigns listed are titular; effective rule was the Governor-General / Viceroy.

name: Chalukyas of Badami

rulers.name: Pulakeshin II
rulers.notes: Defeated Harsha on Narmada; received Persian envoy

notable_achievements: Cave temples at Badami and Aihole
Pulakeshin II defeated Harsha (only major check on Harshavardhana)
Aihole inscription of Ravikirti

name: Western Chalukyas
region: Deccan (Karnataka, parts of Maharashtra and Andhra)

rulers.name: Vikramaditya VI
rulers.notes: Greatest ruler; subject of Bilhana's Vikramankadevacharita; founded Chalukya-Vikrama era

rulers.name: Someshvara IV
rulers.notes: Last; overthrown by Kalachuris and feudatories

notable_achievements: Patronage of Kannada literature (Pampa II, Ranna)
Lingayat (Virashaiva) movement under Basavanna emerged at Kalyani
Long wars with Cholas over Vengi

name: Chola Dynasty (Imperial)
region: South India (Tamil Nadu, Sri Lanka, SE Asian coast)

rulers.name: Vijayalaya
rulers.notes: Founder of imperial Cholas; captured Thanjavur

rulers.name: Parantaka I
rulers.notes: Conquered Pandya and Sri Lanka territory

rulers.name: Rajendra I
rulers.notes: Naval expedition to SE Asia (Srivijaya); marched to Ganga

notable_achievements: Brihadishvara Temple (UNESCO)
Only Indian dynasty to invade SE Asia (Srivijaya, 1025)
Sophisticated village self-government documented in Uttaramerur inscription
Bronze sculpture (Nataraja)

name: Gupta Empire

rulers.name: Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya)
rulers.notes: Defeated Western Kshatrapas; court of Kalidasa

notable_achievements: Indian "Golden Age": Kalidasa, Aryabhata, Varahamihira, decimal place-value
Nalanda Mahavihara founded
Hindu temple architecture standardised

name: Gurjara-Pratihara Dynasty

rulers.name: Mihira Bhoja
rulers.notes: Greatest Pratihara; empire from Sutlej to Narmada

rulers.name: Mahipala I
rulers.notes: Sack of Kannauj by Indra III Rashtrakuta

rulers.name: Rajyapala
rulers.notes: Fled Mahmud of Ghazni; killed by Chandella

notable_achievements: Buffer against Arab expansion eastward
Tripartite struggle (with Palas and Rashtrakutas) for Kannauj
notes: Partial ruler list; tripartite-struggle period spans century of Indian history.

name: Haryanka Dynasty

rulers.name: Bimbisara
rulers.notes: Contemporary of the Buddha; expanded Magadha

rulers.name: Ajatashatru
rulers.notes: Patricide; built first fort at Pataliputra

notable_achievements: Established Magadha as a major power
Founded Pataliputra
notes: Earliest historically attested dynasty of Magadha; dates approximate per traditional Buddhist chronology.

name: Hoysala Dynasty

rulers.name: Bittideva (Vishnuvardhana)
rulers.notes: Convert from Jainism to Vaishnavism (Ramanuja); built Chennakeshava at Belur

rulers.name: Veera Ballala II
rulers.notes: Asserted full sovereignty; defeated Yadavas and Cholas

religion: Hinduism (Jainism early; later Vaishnava under Ramanuja)
notable_achievements: Hoysala temples at Belur, Halebidu, Somanathapura (UNESCO)
Patronage of Kannada literature (Janna, Harihara, Raghavanka)
notes: Partial ruler list; major rulers only.

name: Kakatiya Dynasty

rulers.name: Rudrama Devi
rulers.notes: One of few queens regnant in Indian history; praised by Marco Polo

notable_achievements: Warangal Fort and Thousand Pillar Temple
Rudrama Devi — one of India's few sovereign queens

name: Kanva Dynasty

rulers.name: Susharman
rulers.notes: Last; overthrown by Satavahanas per Puranas

notable_achievements: Brief Brahmin dynasty bridging Shunga and Satavahana periods
notes: Sparse epigraphic record; dates per Puranic king-lists.

name: Khalji Dynasty
region: Most of subcontinent except deep south and east

rulers.name: Alauddin Khalji
rulers.notes: Greatest Khalji; price controls, market reforms; conquered Gujarat, Rajasthan, Deccan

notable_achievements: Repelled multiple Mongol invasions
Market and revenue reforms (Diwan-i-Riyasat)
Malik Kafur's campaigns reached the deep south

name: Kushan Empire
region: Central Asia, Afghanistan, north-west India to Varanasi

rulers.name: Kanishka I
rulers.notes: Greatest ruler; patron of Mahayana Buddhism; Fourth Buddhist Council

notable_achievements: Gandhara art
Spread of Mahayana Buddhism to Central Asia and China
Trans-Eurasian Silk Road trade
notes: Kanishka era start contested; the date 127 CE is the current scholarly consensus (Falk 2001).

name: Lodi Dynasty

rulers.name: Ibrahim Lodi
rulers.notes: Last; killed at First Battle of Panipat by Babur

notable_achievements: First Afghan dynasty in India
Foundation of Agra

name: Mamluk Dynasty (Slave Dynasty)
notable_achievements: First Islamic sultanate in India
Qutb Minar and Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque
Razia Sultana — only female sultan
notes: Part of the Delhi Sultanate — five successive dynasties (Mamluk, Khalji, Tughlaq, Sayyid, Lodi) over 1206–1526.

name: Maratha Empire
region: Most of subcontinent (peak); from Attock to Tanjore

rulers.name: Shahu I
rulers.notes: Peshwa office became hereditary under him

rulers.name: Ramaraja
rulers.notes: Nominal; real power with Peshwas at Pune

rulers.name: Pratapsinh
rulers.notes: Last sovereign Chhatrapati; deposed by British after Third Anglo-Maratha War

notable_achievements: Successfully resisted and then supplanted Mughal power across most of India
Naval strength under Kanhoji Angre
Confederation of Peshwa-Scindia-Gaikwad-Holkar-Bhonsle
notes: After Shahu I, real power passed to the Peshwas (Bajirao I, Balaji Bajirao, etc.). Third Battle of Panipat (1761) was a major setback. Confederation broke up in three Anglo-Maratha Wars (1775-1818).

name: Mauryan Empire

rulers.name: Ashoka the Great
rulers.notes: Embraced Buddhism after Kalinga War; dhamma edicts

rulers.name: Brihadratha
rulers.notes: Last Maurya; assassinated by Pushyamitra Shunga

religion: Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism (pluralistic; Ashoka patronised Buddhism)
notable_achievements: First pan-Indian empire
Ashoka's rock and pillar edicts
Spread of Buddhism to Sri Lanka and Central Asia

name: Mughal Empire
region: Most of the Indian subcontinent (peak under Aurangzeb)

rulers.name: Humayun
rulers.notes: First reign; lost to Sher Shah Suri

rulers.name: Shah Jahan
rulers.notes: Architectural peak; Taj Mahal, Red Fort

rulers.name: Aurangzeb (Alamgir)
rulers.notes: Maximum extent; Deccan wars exhausted empire

rulers.name: Shah Alam II
rulers.notes: Battle of Buxar; British protectorate from 1765

rulers.name: Bahadur Shah II (Zafar)
rulers.notes: Last Mughal; exiled by British after 1857 revolt

notable_achievements: Taj Mahal, Red Fort, Fatehpur Sikri (UNESCO)
Persian as administrative language
Mughal miniature painting
Akbar's policy of sulh-i-kul
notes: Partial ruler list — 19 emperors total. Imperial power effectively ended after Aurangzeb (1707); successors increasingly nominal.

name: Kingdom of Mysore (Wadiyar & Sultanate)

rulers.name: Yaduraya Wadiyar
rulers.notes: Founder of Wadiyar line; Vijayanagara feudatory

rulers.name: Hyder Ali
rulers.notes: De facto ruler; usurper; First and Second Anglo-Mysore Wars

rulers.name: Tipu Sultan
rulers.notes: Killed at fall of Srirangapatna; pioneered war rockets

rulers.name: Krishnaraja Wadiyar III
rulers.notes: British restored Wadiyar line as princely state

notable_achievements: Tipu Sultan's war rockets (precursor to modern artillery rockets)
Long Wadiyar continuity from Vijayanagara feudatory to Indian Republic
notes: Partial ruler list across 25+ Wadiyars plus the Sultanate interregnum. Princely state status 1799–1947.

name: Nanda Dynasty

rulers.name: Dhana Nanda
rulers.notes: Last Nanda; overthrown by Chandragupta Maurya

notable_achievements: Largest Indian empire before the Mauryas
Standing army recorded by Greek sources
notes: Tradition records eight sons of Mahapadma; only first and last are securely dated.

name: Pala Empire
region: Bengal, Bihar, parts of Odisha and Assam

rulers.name: Dharmapala
rulers.notes: Founded Vikramashila; tripartite struggle for Kannauj

rulers.name: Devapala
rulers.notes: Greatest Pala; empire extended to Assam and Odisha

notable_achievements: Last major royal patrons of Indian Buddhism
Founded Vikramashila and patronised Nalanda
Spread of Buddhism to Tibet (Atisha)
notes: Partial ruler list; ~18 Palas known.

name: Pallava Dynasty
region: South India (northern Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh)

rulers.name: Mahendravarman I
rulers.notes: Patron of rock-cut architecture; convert to Shaivism

rulers.name: Narasimhavarman I (Mamalla)
rulers.notes: Sacked Chalukya capital Vatapi; Mahabalipuram monuments

rulers.name: Aparajitavarman
rulers.notes: Last; defeated by Chola Aditya I

notable_achievements: Mahabalipuram rock-cut monuments (UNESCO)
Origin of Dravidian temple architecture
Patronised Tamil and Sanskrit literature
notes: Early Pallavas (3rd–6th c.) are poorly attested; ruler list focuses on the imperial phase from Simhavishnu onwards.

name: Pandyan Dynasty (Later)

rulers.name: Maravarman Sundara Pandyan I
rulers.notes: Re-established Pandyan sovereignty after Chola dominance

rulers.name: Maravarman Kulasekara Pandyan I
rulers.notes: Marco Polo visited; succession war led to Khalji invasion

religion: Hinduism (Shaiva, Vaishnava); Jainism, Buddhism patronised in earlier periods
notable_achievements: Brief imperial revival in south India between Cholas and Vijayanagara
Madurai temple expansion
notes: The Pandyan name covers multiple non-contiguous dynasties from Sangam era (3rd c. BCE) onwards; this record covers the later imperial phase.

name: Pushyabhuti Dynasty

rulers.name: Harshavardhana
rulers.notes: Unified north India; patron of Xuanzang and Bana

notable_achievements: Last great empire of northern India before the Turkic conquest
Patronage of Bana's Harshacharita and Xuanzang's visit

name: Rashtrakuta Dynasty
region: Deccan (most of modern Maharashtra, Karnataka, AP, parts of north India)

rulers.name: Dhruva Dharavarsha
rulers.notes: First Rashtrakuta to win the tripartite struggle

rulers.name: Govinda III
rulers.notes: Greatest military success; defeated Palas and Pratiharas

rulers.name: Amoghavarsha I
rulers.notes: Patron of Jainism; author of Kavirajamarga (earliest Kannada poetics)

rulers.name: Indra IV
rulers.notes: Last; took sallekhana (Jain ritual fast unto death)

notable_achievements: Kailasa Temple at Ellora (monolithic)
Amoghavarsha's Kavirajamarga — first work in Kannada literature
Arab geographers ranked Rashtrakutas among "Four Great Kingdoms" of the world

name: Satavahana Dynasty
notable_achievements: Patronage of Amaravati and Nagarjunakonda Buddhist sites
First Deccan dynasty to issue lead and silver coinage
Maritime trade with Rome
notes: Partial ruler list; ~30 kings known from Puranas, ~17 epigraphically attested.

name: Sayyid Dynasty
notable_achievements: Held a much-diminished Delhi Sultanate together for 37 years
notes: Claimed descent from Prophet Muhammad; minor dynasty by territorial extent.

name: Sena Dynasty

rulers.name: Lakshmana Sena
rulers.notes: Patron of Jayadeva; fled Bakhtiyar Khilji 1204

notable_achievements: Patronage of Jayadeva (Gita Govinda)
Codification of Bengali social structure

name: Seuna (Yadava) Dynasty

rulers.name: Bhillama V
rulers.notes: Founder of sovereign Yadava line; built Devagiri fort

rulers.name: Singhana II
rulers.notes: Greatest Yadava; patron of Sangita Ratnakara

rulers.name: Ramachandra
rulers.notes: Defeated by Alauddin Khalji 1296; vassal thereafter

notable_achievements: Patronage of Marathi (Jnaneshwar, Mukundaraja)
Sangita Ratnakara (foundational music treatise)

name: Shishunaga Dynasty

rulers.name: Mahanandin
rulers.notes: Last ruler; overthrown by Mahapadma Nanda

notable_achievements: Destroyed Avanti rival kingdom
Hosted Second Buddhist Council
notes: Dates per Buddhist chronology; ruler list may be incomplete between Kalashoka and Mahanandin.

name: Shunga Dynasty

rulers.name: Devabhuti
rulers.notes: Last Shunga; killed by Vasudeva Kanva

notable_achievements: Stupa at Bharhut
Revival of Sanskrit and Brahmanical ritual
Patanjali wrote Mahabhashya

name: Sikh Empire
region: Punjab, Kashmir, Khyber, parts of Tibet

rulers.name: Maharaja Ranjit Singh
rulers.notes: Founder; unified Sikh misls; modernised army with European officers

rulers.name: Duleep Singh
rulers.notes: Last; annexed by British after Second Anglo-Sikh War

notable_achievements: Only Indian power to push Khyber boundary west of Indus
Modernised army (Khalsa Fauj) under European generals (Allard, Ventura)
Last independent Indian polity before British annexation of Punjab

name: Sur Empire

rulers.name: Sher Shah Suri
rulers.notes: Founder; defeated Humayun; remarkable administrative reformer

notable_achievements: Sher Shah's revenue system (basis for Akbar's)
Grand Trunk Road revival
Rupiya silver coin (origin of modern rupee)
notes: Interregnum between Humayun's first and second Mughal reigns. Brief, but Sher Shah's reforms shaped Mughal administration.

name: Kingdom of Travancore
region: Southern Kerala, parts of Tamil Nadu

rulers.name: Marthanda Varma
rulers.notes: Founder of modern Travancore; defeated Dutch at Colachel 1741

notable_achievements: Battle of Colachel (1741) — only Indian defeat of a European naval force
High literacy and education reforms
Princely state with progressive administration
notes: Partial ruler list — major rulers only.

name: Tughlaq Dynasty

rulers.name: Muhammad bin Tughlaq
rulers.notes: Capital shift to Daulatabad; token currency; ambitious failures

notable_achievements: Maximum geographic extent of any Delhi Sultanate dynasty (briefly)
Token currency experiment by Muhammad bin Tughlaq
notes: Timur's sack of Delhi in 1398 effectively broke Tughlaq power.

name: Vakataka Dynasty

rulers.name: Rudrasena II
rulers.notes: Married Prabhavatigupta, daughter of Chandragupta II

notable_achievements: Patronage of Ajanta caves (Vakataka phase)
Marriage alliance with Imperial Guptas
notes: Two branches: Pravarapura-Nandivardhana (main) and Vatsagulma. Ruler list is for the main line.

name: Vijayanagara Empire
region: South India (most of peninsula south of Krishna)

rulers.name: Krishnadevaraya
rulers.notes: Greatest ruler; Tuluva dynasty; author of Amuktamalyada

notable_achievements: Hampi (UNESCO) — largest medieval Hindu capital
Defended south India from northern Sultanates for ~230 years
Krishnadevaraya — patron of Telugu Astadiggajas
notes: Four successive dynasties: Sangama (1336–1485), Saluva (1485–1505), Tuluva (1491–1570), Aravidu (1542–1646). Battle of Talikota (1565) effectively ended its peak.

name: Western Kshatrapas

rulers.name: Nahapana
rulers.notes: Issued silver coins; defeated by Gautamiputra Satakarni

rulers.name: Rudradaman I
rulers.notes: Junagadh rock inscription; first long Sanskrit prashasti

rulers.name: Rudrasimha III
rulers.notes: Last; killed by Chandragupta II of Gupta dynasty

notable_achievements: Earliest long Sanskrit inscription (Junagadh, 150 CE)
Long-running silver coinage
notes: Partial ruler list; over two dozen Kshatrapas known. Two parallel branches (Kshaharata then Kardamaka).
